name: Puerto del Espino and La Torre de les Maçanes – loop from Busot
name_original: Puerto del Espino y La Torre de les Maçanes — Circular desde Busot
description:
This route is a variant of the Busot Iron Gravel Sprint that starts from the public car park of the municipal school. Although the distance is not very long, the almost 1,300 metres of positive gradient make it a good sporting challenge. On the other hand, there is only one pass through town, in this case, La Torre de les Maçanes (Torremanzanas), at the other end, so remember to bring enough water and food.

The route mostly runs along dirt tracks or rough asphalt. One of the highlights of the day is the climb to Puerto del Espino, which has concrete ramps whose surface is a clear indicator of the gradient. What is guaranteed are the endless views that disappear over infinite blue mountain ridges.

The paths disappear into almost virgin landscapes and the visions of cliffs and crags follow one another at every bend in the road. You also cross areas of cultivated fields, but very little frequented by people, which increases the chances of wildlife sightings. Keep an eye out for goats, roe deer, partridges and hares that you may see along the way. In Busot you have several restaurants where you can enjoy a well-deserved treat at the end of the route.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Busot offers diverse terrain, from asphalt and narrow roads to challenging forest tracks and gravel climbs. The region is characterized by its position at the foot of the Cabeçó d'Or mountain, featuring mountain ranges, valleys, and varied natural landscapes. Cyclists can expect significant elevation changes and a mix of unpaved and difficult segments. Busot's strategic location provides a blend of mountain scenery and proximity to the Mediterranean coast. Best gravel bike trails around Busot The most popular gravel bike trail is Puerto del Espino and La Torre de les Maçanes – loop from Busot, a 35.8 miles (57.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 7 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Comptador Pass – Historic Center of Orxeta loop from Busot, a difficult 65.8 miles (105.9 km) path. This route traverses mountain passes and offers extensive views of the surrounding landscape. Local gravel bikers also love the Beautiful stretch of beach – Backroad from Xixona to Busot loop from el Campello, a 37.7 miles (60.7 km) trail leading through varied scenery including coastal areas and inland backroads, often completed in about 4 hours 58 minutes. Gravel biking around Busot is defined by mountain ranges, valleys, and diverse road surfaces including forest tracks and challenging climbs. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on difficult routes. The routes in Busot are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 11 reviews. More than 278 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Busot's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available around Busot?
A: There are over 25 gravel bike trails around Busot listed on komoot, offering a wide range of experiences for cyclists. The region is known for its challenging routes, with the majority being classified as difficult.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for gravel biking in Busot?
A: Gravel biking in Busot features diverse terrain, including asphalt, narrow roads, challenging forest tracks, and significant gravel climbs and descents. You'll encounter varied landscapes, from mountain ranges and valleys to areas near the Mediterranean coast.

Q: Are there any easy gravel bike routes for beginners in Busot?
A: While Busot is primarily known for its more challenging gravel routes, with 24 out of 27 routes rated as difficult, there are a few moderate options available. Beginners should be prepared for varied surfaces and elevation changes even on these routes.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints I can expect to see on a gravel ride in Busot?
A: Busot's gravel routes often pass through spectacular areas. You can expect breathtaking views from mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2838223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Busot&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2604644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Pass&lt;/a&gt;. The region also offers stunning vistas from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4631915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Relleu Footbridge Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3576092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeçó d'Or Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes starting from Busot?
A: Yes, many gravel routes in Busot are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1922710283/puerto-del-espino-and-la-torre-de-les-macanes-loop-from-busot" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Espino and La Torre de les Maçanes loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option starting from Busot.

Q: What do other gravel bikers say about the routes in Busot?
A: The gravel biking routes in Busot are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 11 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, challenging climbs, and the spectacular views of the surrounding mountains and valleys.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the gravel trails in Busot?
A: Yes, you can incorporate historical elements into your ride. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40018180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Castillo de Busot – Busot Town Center loop&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the historic Busot Castle ruins. The region also features the famous Canelobre Caves, a significant natural and historical attraction near the town.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Busot?
A: Busot's climate makes it suitable for gravel biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures, while winter can also be a good option with cooler, drier weather. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during those months.

Q: Can I find long-distance gravel cycling routes around Busot?
A: Absolutely. Busot offers several longer routes for experienced gravel bikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38135690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comptador Pass – Historic Center of Orxeta loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a demanding 105.9 km path that traverses mountain passes and provides extensive views, requiring good fitness.

Q: Are there any gravel routes that offer a mix of mountain and coastal scenery?
A: Yes, Busot's strategic location allows for routes that blend mountain and coastal landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40013635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful stretch of beach – Backroad from Xixona to Busot loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading through varied scenery including coastal areas and inland backroads.

Q: Is it possible to access gravel trails in Busot using public transport?
A: Busot is approximately 20 kilometers north of Alicante city. While direct public transport options to specific trailheads might be limited, you can reach Busot town by bus from Alicante. From there, many routes start directly or are a short ride away.

Q: Are there any family-friendly gravel biking options in Busot?
A: Given that most routes in Busot are rated as difficult, truly family-friendly gravel options suitable for young children or beginners are limited. However, families with older, experienced riders might find some of the shorter, less technical sections enjoyable, though significant elevation changes are common.
tips: It is a 12 km long mountain pass from Busot, with long and extended slopes that extend up to 17%, with semi-asphalted sections. The descent towards Torremanzanas is paved and is very easy to go down. It is not technical, it is a track in very good condition, the only difficulty is its vertical climbs.
This is a charming village at the foot of the mountains. It has a great cycling atmosphere thanks to the influx of cycling enthusiasts. It is ideal for a gastronomic break.
bad road surface but very nice with the gravel bike. No traffic here, you can cycle very quietly.
